summary: Add min-observation state to deferred ideas before promotion.
why: The current Deferred/Promoted convention needs an explicit corroboration threshold so single sightings remain parked and repeated sightings auto-promote.

summary: Persist idea rejections as bounded JSONL state.
why: Markdown-only rejection can be lost or reintroduced on resume; typed idea_rejected records give the workflow a durable suppression key.

summary: Gate idea promotion in the auto workflow before next-focus selection.
why: The auto workflow reads ratios and strategy focus but not a structured ideas index; admission should drop rejected ideas and only promote threshold-qualified ones.

summary: Leaf agent should count matching ideas or suppress rejected matches.
why: When a tangent matches an existing deferred idea, the agent should add corroboration; when it matches a rejected idea, it should record suppression rather than append a duplicate.

summary: Reducer should rank deferred ideas and emit promotion candidates.
why: Keep leaf iterations append-only; let the reducer build the ideas index, suppress rejected fingerprints, and surface deterministic promotion candidates.

summary: S2-03 and S2-05 already covered the generic threshold and rejection mechanisms; this iteration adds new target mapping for the ideas backlog lifecycle.
The active packet does not currently contain research/research-ideas.md, so the backlog recommendation targets reusable workflow contracts rather than packet-local content.
